title: World History
description: Question batch for the Contemporary History domain.

questions.question: Which event began in 1914 and became World War I?
questions.explanation: The assassination in Sarajevo triggered World War I.

questions.question: Which treaty formally ended World War I for Germany?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Versailles ended the war for Germany.

questions.question: Which revolution brought the Bolsheviks to power in Russia?
questions.explanation: The Bolsheviks took power in the October Revolution of 1917.

questions.question: Which global economic crisis began in 1929?
questions.explanation: The Great Depression began after the 1929 crash.

questions.question: Which war began when Germany invaded Poland in 1939?
questions.explanation: Germany's invasion of Poland started World War II in Europe.

questions.question: Which event brought the United States into World War II?
questions.explanation: Japan attacked Pearl Harbor in 1941.

questions.question: Which organization was founded in 1945 to promote peace?
questions.explanation: The United Nations was founded in 1945.

questions.question: What was the Cold War mainly?
answers: A rivalry between the US and USSR
questions.explanation: The Cold War was a global rivalry between superpowers.

questions.question: Which wall fell in 1989 as Cold War tensions eased?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell in 1989.

questions.question: Which state dissolved in 1991?
questions.explanation: The Soviet Union dissolved in 1991.

questions.question: Which policy helped rebuild Western Europe after World War II?
questions.explanation: The Marshall Plan funded European recovery.

questions.question: Which trials prosecuted Nazi leaders after World War II?
questions.explanation: The Nuremberg Trials prosecuted key Nazi figures.

questions.question: Which 1962 crisis brought the US and USSR close to nuclear war?
questions.explanation: The Cuban Missile Crisis was a peak Cold War confrontation.

questions.question: Which conflict divided Korea after World War II?
questions.explanation: The Korean War hardened Korea's division.

questions.question: Which war drew heavy US involvement in Southeast Asia?
questions.explanation: The United States became deeply involved in the Vietnam War.

questions.question: Which movement challenged racial segregation in the US?
questions.explanation: The Civil Rights Movement fought segregation and discrimination.

questions.question: Which policy in the USSR is linked to Mikhail Gorbachev?
questions.explanation: Glasnost encouraged greater openness.

questions.question: Which European conflict in the 1990s followed Yugoslavia's breakup?
questions.explanation: The breakup of Yugoslavia led to Balkan conflicts.

questions.question: Which event in 2001 deeply shaped global security policy?
questions.explanation: The September 11 attacks reshaped security policy worldwide.

questions.question: Which process describes former colonies gaining independence?
questions.explanation: Decolonization describes colonies becoming independent states.

questions.question: Why did World War I become so destructive?
questions.explanation: Modern weapons and trench warfare caused huge casualties.

questions.question: Why was appeasement later criticized in the 1930s?
questions.explanation: Appeasement did not prevent further expansion by dictatorships.

questions.question: What was the Holocaust?
answers: The systematic murder of Jews and others by Nazi Germany
questions.explanation: The Holocaust was Nazi Germany's genocide.

questions.question: Why was D-Day important?
answers: It opened a western front in Europe
questions.explanation: The 1944 Normandy invasion helped liberate Western Europe.

questions.question: What did containment aim to do?
questions.explanation: Containment aimed to stop communist expansion.

questions.question: Why was the space race important?
answers: It symbolized Cold War technological rivalry
questions.explanation: The space race became a symbol of superpower competition.

questions.question: What was apartheid?
answers: A system of racial segregation in South Africa
questions.explanation: Apartheid enforced racial segregation in South Africa.

questions.question: Which trend accelerated after World War II?
questions.explanation: Trade, communication, and interdependence grew globally.

questions.question: Why did many Asian and African states seek independence after 1945?
answers: Colonial empires weakened and nationalist movements grew
questions.explanation: Postwar weakness and nationalism drove decolonization.

questions.question: What best describes the post-1991 world order?
answers: Less bipolar than the Cold War era
questions.explanation: After 1991, the strict US-USSR bipolar structure ended.
